title: A Study to Investigate the Safety and Efficacy of SAR446523 Injected Subcutaneously in Adult Participants With Relapsed/Refractory Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Part A allows prior GPRC5D and BCMA exposure. Part B excludes prior GPRC5D therapy. Prior NK-cell engaging therapy within 90 days is excluded. Inadequate organ/marrow function and significant concomitant illness are exclusion criteria.

title: Integrative Therapies in Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Trial restricted to patients able to communicate in Hebrew, Arabic, or Russian. Exclusion of platelet count <20×10⁹/L for acupuncture patients and DVT exclusion for reflexology patients suggest supportive care interventions rather than drug trial. Hemodynamic instability and psychiatric impairment are exclusion criteria.

title: Isatuximab in Combination With Novel Agents in RRMM - Master Protocol

eligibility.notes: Anti-CD38 therapy eligibility varies by substudy: Substudies 01/06 require 12-month washout if prior exposed; Substudies 02/03 require 6-month washout if prior exposed but not refractory; Substudy 04 requires prior anti-CD38 and anti-BCMA exposure; Substudy 05 requires prior anti-CD38 exposure and mandatory anti-BCMA exposure if available.

title: Study of ABBV-383 Assessing Adverse Events and Clinical Activity With Subcutaneous (SC) Injection in Adult Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Participants must be naïve to ABBV-383 treatment. Prior triple class exposure (PI, anti-CD38, IMiD) is required for inclusion.

title: A Study to Assess Adverse Events of Intravenously (IV) Infused ABBV-383 in Adult Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Multi-arm trial with arm-specific eligibility: Arm A requires ≥3 lines (PI, IMiD, anti-CD38), excludes prior BCMA; Arm B requires ≥2 lines including prior BCMA-targeted therapy (ADC or CAR-T); Arm C requires ≥2 lines (PI, IMiD, anti-CD38), max ECOG 1, excludes rapidly progressing disease; Arm D requires 1-3 lines (exposure to PI, IMiD, or anti-CD38), max ECOG 1, excludes rapidly progressing disease.

title: A Study of JNJ-79635322 in Combination With Daratumumab or Pomalidomide for Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Treatment regimen B requires lenalidomide refractoriness or ≥2 prior lines. Allogeneic SCT exclusion within 6 months; autologous SCT exclusion within 12 weeks. Prior anti-CD38 therapy washout is 90 days for regimen A and 21 days for regimen B. Prior CD3-redirecting therapy is excluded.

title: Study Assessing Activity of Intravenous (IV) Etentamig Monotherapy Versus Standard Available Therapies in Adult Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Clinically significant drug or alcohol abuse within last 6 months is exclusionary. Clinically significant neurologic, psychiatric, endocrine, metabolic, immunologic, cardiovascular, pulmonary, or hepatic disease within last 6 months is exclusionary.

title: Study of CAR-BCMA, a Chimeric Antigen Receptor T Cell (CAR-T) Therapy Directed Against BCMA in Subjects With Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Cardiac ejection fraction ≥45% required within 6 weeks of treatment start. Systemic corticosteroids >5 mg/day prednisone not allowed within 2 weeks prior to leukapheresis or conditioning regimen, and within 2 weeks prior to and 30 days after CAR T-cell infusion.

title: MagnetisMM-32: A Study to Learn About the Study Medicine Called Elranatamab in People With Multiple Myeloma (MM) That Has Come Back After Taking Other Treatments (Including Prior Treatment With an Anti-CD38 Antibody and Lenalidomide)

eligibility.notes: Prior ASCT allowed but graft-versus-host disease must be inactive. Smoldering MM is excluded. Progressive disease required.

title: Study With Elranatamab Versus Lenalidomide in Patients With Newly Diagnosed Multiple Myeloma After Transplant

eligibility.notes: Part 1 requires MRD positivity; Part 2 allows MRD negative or positive status. Randomization window: within 120 days post-ASCT, or within 60 days of consolidation therapy and within 7 months from ASCT. Must achieve partial response or better at randomization. Previous MM maintenance therapy is exclusionary.

title: A Study to Compare Iberdomide Maintenance Versus Lenalidomide Maintenance Therapy Following Autologous Stem Cell Transplant in Participants With Newly Diagnosed Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Patients must have received 3-6 cycles of induction therapy including PI and IMiD with or without anti-CD38, followed by single or tandem ASCT. Enrollment must be within 12 months (single) or 15 months (tandem) from induction initiation with at least PR response post-ASCT. Patients with progressive disease or clinical relapse after ASCT are excluded.

title: Open-label Study Comparing Iberdomide, Daratumumab and Dexamethasone (IberDd) Versus Daratumumab, Bortezomib, and Dexamethasone (DVd) in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma (RRMM)

eligibility.notes: Patients must have documented disease progression during or after their last anti-myeloma regimen (indicating relapsed/refractory disease).

title: A Study to Examine the Effects of Novel Therapy Linvoseltamab in Combination With Other Cancer Treatments for Adult Patients With Multiple Myeloma That is Resistant to Current Standard of Care Treatments

eligibility.notes: Cohort-specific criteria vary regarding PI/IMiD/anti-CD38 exposure and refractoriness; most require 6-month washout from prior therapy (longer than general 21-day minimum). Prior CAR-T or T cell-based immunotherapy excluded per incomplete final criterion.

title: A Study to Learn About the Effects of the Combination of Elranatamab (PF-06863135), Daratumumab, Lenalidomide or Elranatamab and Lenalidomide Compared With Daratumumab, Lenalidomide, and Dexamethasone in Patients With Newly Diagnosed Multiple Myeloma Who Are Not Candidates for Transplant

eligibility.notes: Part 1: RRMM with 1-2 prior lines (including ≥1 IMiD and ≥1 PI) OR NDMM transplant-ineligible. Part 2: NDMM transplant-ineligible or transplant-deferred. For RRMM: prior BCMA or anti-CD38 therapy within 6 months excluded; stem cell transplant ≤3 months or active GVHD excluded. For NDMM: only short course corticosteroids allowed prior; SMM excluded.

title: A Study of Daratumumab, Bortezomib, Lenalidomide and Dexamethasone (DVRd) Followed by Ciltacabtagene Autoleucel Versus Daratumumab, Bortezomib, Lenalidomide and Dexamethasone (DVRd) Followed by Autologous Stem Cell Transplant (ASCT) in Participants With Newly Diagnosed Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Treatment-naive requirement: any prior MM therapy other than short course of corticosteroids is excluded. Stroke or seizure within 6 months is an exclusion criterion. CYP3A4 inducer washout of 5 half-lives required.

title: A Clinical Trial of Four Medicines (Elranatamab Plus Carfilzomib and Dexamethasone or Maplirpacept) in People With Relapsed Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Part 2 requires ≥3 prior lines and refractoriness to at least one IMiD, one PI, and one anti-CD38 antibody. Part 1 excludes prior BCMA therapy; Part 2 excludes prior anti-BCMA therapy except CAR-T, and excludes CD47-SIRP alpha therapy. ASCT within 12 weeks prior or active GVHD is exclusionary.

title: Phase 3 Study of Teclistamab in Combination With Lenalidomide and Teclistamab Alone Versus Lenalidomide Alone in Participants With Newly Diagnosed Multiple Myeloma as Maintenance Therapy Following Autologous Stem Cell Transplantation

eligibility.notes: Patients must have achieved at least partial response (≥PR) after one line of induction +/- consolidation therapy without progression. Patients intolerant to lenalidomide or who discontinued due to lenalidomide-related AE are excluded. Live attenuated vaccines within 4 weeks prior to first treatment dose are prohibited.

title: A Study of JNJ-68284528, a Chimeric Antigen Receptor T Cell (CAR-T) Therapy Directed Against B-cell Maturation Antigen (BCMA) in Participants With Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Multi-cohort trial with varying inclusion criteria by cohort. Cohort A: 1-3 prior lines with PI/IMiD exposure, lenalidomide refractory. Cohort B: 1 prior line with PI/IMiD, PD ≤12 months post-ASCT or from therapy start. Cohort C: Prior exposure to PI, IMiD, anti-CD38, BCMA. Cohort D: NDMM with 4-8 cycles including ASCT. Cohort E: NDMM, treatment naive (≤1 cycle acceptable), high-risk features required. Cohort F: NDMM with VGPR or better response, 4-8 cycles of specified regimens (D-VRd, D-Rd, or carfilzomib-based). Cohort G: NDMM ineligible for ASCT due to age/comorbidities/refusal. Cohort H: NDMM ASCT candidate. Exclusion text incomplete in provided criteria.

title: A Study of Teclistamab in Combination With Daratumumab and Lenalidomide (Tec-DR) and Talquetamab in Combination With Daratumumab and Lenalidomide (Tal-DR) in Participants With Newly Diagnosed Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Participants must not be candidates for ASCT due to advanced age, comorbidities, or planned deferral. Prior corticosteroid use allowed (≤160 mg dexamethasone total, or ≥20 mg cumulative within 14 days before randomization). Known contraindications to daratumumab or lenalidomide per local prescribing information are exclusionary.

title: A Long-term Study for Participants Previously Treated With Ciltacabtagene Autoleucel

eligibility.notes: This appears to be a follow-up study for participants who previously received cilta-cel in Company-sponsored trials. No exclusion criteria provided in the text.

title: A Study of Selinexor (Seli) + Low-dose Dexamethasone (LDD) in Penta-refractory Multiple Myeloma (MM), Seli and Bortezomib + LDD in Triple-class Refractory MM.

eligibility.notes: SVd arm has greater than Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy or Grade ≥2 PN with pain exclusion. Prior treatment requirements vary by arm (3-5 lines for different arms); parsing reflects most permissive SVd arm criteria (1-5 prior therapies). Active graft versus host disease is an exclusion criterion.

title: Cyclosporine in Combination With Carfilzomib and Dexamethasone in Relapsed Multiple Myeloma Refractory to Carfilzomib and High Expression of PPIA Gene in Myeloma Cells

eligibility.notes: Patients must have received exactly 2 prior lines of therapy including bortezomib, lenalidomide, and daratumumab. Most recent therapy must be carfilzomib-based (within 3 months of enrollment) with failure to achieve minor response or refractory status. Prior allogeneic transplantation is an exclusion criterion.

title: A Phase 1b/2 Study of Sonrotoclax (BGB-11417) as Monotherapy and in Various Combinations With Dexamethasone Plus Carfilzomib, Dexamethasone Plus Daratumumab, and Dexamethasone Plus Pomalidomide in Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: t(11;14) translocation confirmation by FISH is required. Different cohorts have varying line of therapy requirements: Part 1 and Part 2 Cohorts 1-2 require ≥3 prior lines with PI, IMiD, anti-CD38; Part 2 Cohorts 3-5 require ≥1 prior line; Part 2 Cohorts 6-7 require 1-3 prior lines with PI and IMiD. Non-secretory MM and solitary plasmacytoma are excluded. Recent cardiac event within 6 months is exclusion criterion.

title: Study of HBI0101 (NXC-201) CAR-T Therapy in Multiple Myeloma and Light-Chain Amyloidosis

eligibility.notes: Trial includes both multiple myeloma and light-chain amyloidosis patients with distinct exposure requirements. LVEF <40% is exclusion criterion. Prior allogeneic SCT allowed if no active GVHD for ≥1 month off immunosuppression.

title: A Study to Evaluate Mezigdomide in Combination With Carfilzomib and Dexamethasone (MeziKD) Versus Carfilzomib and Dexamethasone (Kd) in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma (SUCCESSOR-2)

eligibility.notes: Participants must achieve minimal response or better to at least 1 prior therapy and have documented disease progression during or after last antimyeloma regimen. Prior autologous stem cell transplant allowed if >12 weeks before study initiation; prior allogeneic stem cell transplant is prohibited.

title: A Study to Evaluate Mezigdomide, Bortezomib and Dexamethasone (MEZIVd) Versus Pomalidomide, Bortezomib and Dexamethasone (PVd) in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma (RRMM)

eligibility.notes: Participants must have achieved MR or better to at least 1 prior therapy. PI-refractory defined as progression during treatment or within 60 days of last PI dose, with exceptions for bortezomib maintenance ≤2 weeks frequency or ixazomib monotherapy maintenance ≥6 months prior. Prior bortezomib-containing regimen participants excluded if best response <MR or discontinued due to toxicity.

title: A Study to Compare the Efficacy and Safety of BMS-986393 Versus Standard Regimens in Adult Participants With Relapsed or Refractory and Lenalidomide-refractory Multiple Myeloma (QUINTESSENTIAL-2)

eligibility.notes: Prior exposure to lenalidomide is required. Solitary plasmacytomas and non-secretory myeloma without measurable disease are excluded.

title: A Study Evaluating the Safety, Pharmacokinetics, and Activity of the Combination of Cevostamab and Elranatamab in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma (R/R MM)

eligibility.notes: Prior treatment with FcRH5-targeting agents (cevostamab) or elranatamab is excluded. Significant cardiovascular disease and history of CMV/EBV infection, HLH/MAS, or PML are exclusion criteria.

title: A Trial to Learn How Well Linvoseltamab Works Compared to the Combination of Elotuzumab, Pomalidomide and Dexamethasone for Adult Participants With Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Patients with only 1 prior line must be lenalidomide refractory. Participants in Israel must have prior CD38 antibody; EU/UK participants require 2-4 prior lines including CD38 antibody. Cardiac ejection fraction must be ≥40%. BCMA ADCs (antibody-drug conjugates) are permitted despite BCMA CAR-T exclusion. Prior allogeneic SCT within 6 months or autologous SCT within 12 weeks are exclusions.

title: A Study Comparing Teclistamab Monotherapy Versus Pomalidomide, Bortezomib, Dexamethasone (PVd) or Carfilzomib, Dexamethasone (Kd) in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: Patients must have documented progressive disease or failure to achieve response to last line of therapy. Exclusions for control arms: PVd arm excludes those with prior pomalidomide or Grade 1 PN with pain/Grade ≥2 PN; Kd arm excludes those with prior carfilzomib or uncontrolled hypertension or Grade 2 PN with pain/Grade ≥3 PN.

title: A Study Comparing Talquetamab in Combination With Daratumumab or in Combination With Daratumumab and Pomalidomide Versus Daratumumab in Combination With Pomalidomide and Dexamethasone in Participants With Multiple Myeloma That Returns After Treatment or is Resistant to Treatment

eligibility.notes: Participants with only 1 prior line must be lenalidomide-refractory; participants with ≥2 prior lines must be lenalidomide-exposed. Maximum cumulative corticosteroid dose restricted to ≥140 mg prednisone equivalent within 14-day period before first dose.

title: A Study Comparing Talquetamab Plus Pomalidomide, Talquetamab Plus Teclistamab, and Elotuzumab, Pomalidomide, and Dexamethasone or Pomalidomide, Bortezomib, and Dexamethasone in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Myeloma Who Have Received an Anti-CD38 Antibody and Lenalidomide

eligibility.notes: Stroke, TIA, or seizure within 6 months prior to ICF is exclusion. Major surgery or significant traumatic injury within 2 weeks prior to treatment start is exclusion. Maximum cumulative corticosteroid dose ≥140 mg prednisone equivalent within 14 days before first study drug dose is exclusion.

title: A Study of Talquetamab in Participants With Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma

eligibility.notes: CAR-T/T cell redirection therapy exclusion varies by cohort: Cohorts A and C exclude any prior exposure; Cohorts B, D, and E exclude exposure within 3 months. Corticosteroid limit: ≥140 mg prednisone equivalent within 14 days prior to first dose is excluded.

title: Predicting Progression of Developing Myeloma in a High-Risk Screened Population

eligibility.notes: Trial is for high-risk asymptomatic individuals with family history of plasma cell dyscrasias. Excludes patients already diagnosed with any plasma cell dyscrasia (MGUS, SMM, MM, Waldenström's). Excludes pregnant females.
